name: Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud
title: Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia

compatibilityOverrides.reason: Khashoggi fallout and oil production disagreements strain relationship
Saudi-Iranian sectarian and geopolitical rivalry across the Middle East
Expanding economic ties and oil-for-infrastructure deals
OPEC+ cooperation despite competing interests in energy markets
Sunni Arab alliance and shared regional security interests

biography: Mohammed bin Salman, known as MBS, transformed Saudi Arabia's power structure by sidelining older princes and consolidating near-absolute authority as Crown Prince. His ENTJ drive fuels the ambitious Vision 2030 economic diversification plan—including the NEOM megacity—while maintaining the kingdom's petro-state leverage through OPEC+. Ruthless in eliminating dissent (the Khashoggi affair, the Ritz-Carlton purge), he simultaneously pursues social liberalization, sports investment diplomacy, and a pragmatic warming toward Israel, positioning Saudi Arabia as the indispensable Middle Eastern power broker.
